scope.goal: close out the Go adapter migration preparation lane by validating the frozen ownership boundaries, recording the final limitations, and confirming the preparation artifacts are complete without expanding Go into a live execution runtime

implemented: confirmed Wave 2 through Wave 6 receipts, execution plan updates, and the Wave 6 builder-only/no-owner freeze are all present under the canonical backend preparation lane
validated that Go ownership ends at provider canonicalization, request semantics/control handoff, and boot-time agent metadata registration
validated that TypeScript runtime/core remains the only execution surface for hook, skill, tool, commander, and cowork runtime behavior
closed the preparation lane without introducing a Go-side commander runtime owner or a second semantic truth source
evidence: backend/outputs/runtime/vibe-sessions/2026-04-12_20-48-47-go-adapter-migration-prep/phase-wave2-provider-handoff.json through phase-wave6-builder-only-no-owner-freeze.json record the frozen slice progression
backend/cmd/codeflow-server/main.go:135-169 keeps Go at resolve/build/register plus adapter close, not a persistent runtime owner
backend/internal/commander/commander.go:337-447 remains a builder handoff into adapters RequestSemantics/SendOptions, not a live execution loop
packages/core/src/tool-runtime/__tests__/ToolRuntime.test.ts passes 12 tests, confirming the focused TS runtime boundary regression target still holds
validation_limited: backend/internal/workflow has no package test files, so cleanup validation there is limited to successful package discovery during go test rather than direct test assertions
the cleanup run did not execute a broader full-repo regression beyond the focused Go packages and the focused ToolRuntime vitest target
manual_test: inspect backend/docs/plans/2026-04-12-go-backend-adapter-semantics-migration-execution-plan.md and outputs/runtime/vibe-sessions/2026-04-12_20-48-47-go-adapter-migration-prep/*.json to confirm the requirement/plan/receipt chain is complete
risk: low: the preparation lane is closed with focused validation and clear runtime ownership boundaries, but broader end-to-end execution behavior remains intentionally outside this preparation scope

notes: Go owns backend-facing provider conversion, request semantics, declaration controls handoff, and boot-time agent metadata registration
TypeScript runtime/core still owns HookManager, SkillDispatcher, HeadlessToolRuntime, Commander orchestration, and cowork execution
This cleanup closes the preparation lane; it does not claim the overall Go migration is complete
